title: papaver nudicaule subsp. corydalifolium
intro: Papaver nudicaule subsp. corydalifolium, also known as the small poppy or corydalis poppy, is a subspecies of the poppy plant that is native to the mountainous regions of the western United States and Canada. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its alkaloid content, particularly the presence of berberine. The small poppy is said to provide health benefits such as relief from pain, fever reduction, and improved digestion, making it a popular remedy for various ailments. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
